result:
Both branches work. Verifying no temporary instrumentation was added â€” I added none.

**Root cause**: The lua-parse codegen for `if`/`elseif`/`else` (`test_then_block` at lib.rs:2895 and `ifstat` at lib.rs:2946) had every jump-patch call stubbed out as `TODO(port)`. `cg_emit_order` correctly emitted `OP_LTI`/`OP_LT` followed by `OP_JMP` with offset `NO_JUMP` (-1), but `goiftrue`/`patchtohere`/`concat` were never called. At runtime the VM took the conditional jump on the comparison and computed `pc = pc + (-1) + 1 = pc` â€” infinite loop.

**Fix**: Added Phase-A local implementations in crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs of `cg_jump`, `cg_get_jump`, `cg_fix_jump`, `cg_get_label`, `cg_concat`, `cg_patch_list`, `cg_patch_to_here`, `cg_negate_condition`, `cg_go_if_true` (mirroring `lcode.c`'s `luaK_*` family), and wired them into `test_then_block` (negate the comparison's k bit, emit jump-over-then on else/elseif, patch the jf to the else label) and `ifstat` (patch escapelist to end of if).

**New behavior**: `if 1 < 2 then print("yes") else print("no") end` prints `yes`; `if 2 < 1 then ... end` prints `no`. Original infinite-loop blocker is gone.

tool_input.command: (cargo run -q -p lua-cli -- 'if 1 < 2 then print("yes") else print("no") end' 2>&1 & PID=$!; sleep 20; kill $PID 2>/dev/null; wait $PID 2>/dev/null) | tail -40

cargo run -q -p lua-cli -- 'if 1 < 2 then print("yes") else print("no") end' 2>&1 &
sleep 15
kill %1 2>/dev/null
wait 2>/dev/null
gtimeout 15 cargo run -q -p lua-cli -- 'if 1 < 2 then print("yes") else print("no") end' 2>&1 | tail -30
